companyProfile.title: Company Profile
paragraphs: ELSP (formerly IET) was established in 2018 as an open-access publisher. From day one our team committed to a model rooted in transparency, accountability, and the rapid, cost-effective dissemination of research — believing that the faster trusted findings reach the people who can build on them, the faster society itself advances.

Our mission is to remove the traditional barriers that have long stood between authors and readers, to actively promote technological innovation across the publishing pipeline, and to provide a dependable platform on which researchers can share breakthrough discoveries with peers and policy-makers around the world.

Our ultimate vision is to help create an open, transparent, and innovative publishing ecosystem — one that advances human knowledge and contributes meaningfully to global well-being for the generations that follow.

openAccess.title: Open Access
openAccess.intro: Open access is a fundamental movement for sharing and circulating academic information — making peer-reviewed scholarship freely available to anyone, anywhere, without subscription or paywall.

points.title: Copyright
points.body: Authors retain copyright of their work, which is published under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license (CC BY 4.0).

points.title: Rights
points.body: Users are permitted to copy, distribute, and adapt the work for non-commercial purposes, provided the original work is properly cited.

points.title: License Details
points.body: The published work is free to read, copy, and distribute. Authors retain publishing rights without restrictions, but reuse of images, figures, or other content must credit the original source.

items.title: High Visibility and High Impact
items.body: Extends the citation reach and global impact of academic articles.

items.title: Reuse with Proper Citation
items.body: Allows others to reuse, circulate, and build upon the research.

items.title: Rigorous Peer Review
items.body: Guarantees the academic quality of every article through peer review.

items.title: No Length Limitation
items.body: Articles are published completely as written, with no artificial page limits.

items.title: Rapid Publication
items.body: A continuous publication model ensures fast online availability.

people.name: Aixu Chen
people.bio: A physicist by training with extensive academic experience across condensed-matter and applied optics, Aixu leads ELSP's editorial direction — shaping policy, recruiting subject editors, and ensuring every title upholds the rigor expected of a peer-reviewed open-access publisher.

people.name: Ming Qi
people.bio: With a background in mechanical engineering and several years of hands-on scientific research, Ming oversees day-to-day editorial operations across the journal portfolio, coordinating peer review, copyediting, and the production pipeline that brings accepted manuscripts online.

people.name: Jiajie Liu
people.bio: Trained in physics and seasoned in academic publishing, Jiajie manages submissions, reviewer engagement, and author communications — keeping the path from submission to publication transparent, predictable, and as short as quality allows.

people.name: Deping Zhang
people.bio: Member of the European Academy of Sciences. Deping advises ELSP on long-range editorial strategy and the cultivation of cross-disciplinary research communities.

people.name: Yue Kiat Soon
people.bio: Research Fellow in the Department of Engineering Science at the University of Oxford. Yue Kiat advises on engineering-domain journals and the integration of computational methods into our editorial workflow.

people.name: Jiang Xie
people.bio: Researcher in the International Research Center for Renewable Energy at Xi'an Jiaotong University. Jiang advises ELSP on renewable-energy and sustainability programs across our journal portfolio.
